DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND IT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO ISSUES 2 (PREMISES OR CONTRACTOR LIABILITY) AND 3 (PUNITIVE DAMAGES) AND DENIED AS TO ISSUES 1 (NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT PRODUCTS LIABILITY) AND 4 (LOSS OF CONSORTIUM). THE DEPOSITION TESTIMONY OF MR. DOYLE AND MR. SCIORTINO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. DOYLE WAS EXPOSED TO 9X9 ASBESTOS-CONTAINING AMTICO TILES MANUFACTURED AT THE PLANT LOCATED AT 1985 EAST STATE STREET IN TRENTON, NEW JERSEY. MR SCIORTINO'S TESTIMONY FURTHER CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER THE ASBESTOS IN THE 9X9 TILES WAS SUPPLIED BY DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. ON THE ISSUES OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES AND PREMISES OR CONTRACTOR LIABILITY, DEFENDANT SATISFIED ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION BY PRESENTING PLAINTIFFS' FACTUALLY DEVOID RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND PLAINTIFFS FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-25-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Ana Portillo, Esq. of JACKSON & WALLACE (415-982-6300), appearing for defendant / moving party H.M. Royal, Incorporated
Bryn C. Gallagher, Esq. of CLAPPER, PATTI, SCHWEIZER & MASON (415-332-4262), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party Harold Doyle

RULING:
Defendant H.M. Royal Incorporated's motion for summary judgment and its alternate motion for summary adjudication. Argued and tentative ruling adopted. Defendant's motion for summary judgment is denied and it's alternate motion for summary adjudication is granted as to issues 2 (premises or contractor liability) and 3 (punitive damages) and denied as to issues 1 (negligence and strict products liability) and 4 (loss of consortium). The deposition testimony of Mr. Doyle and Mr. Sciortino create a triable issue whether Mr. Doyle was exposed to 9x9 asbestos-containing Amtico tiles manufactured at the plant located at 1985 East State Street in Trenton, New Jersey. Mr Sciortino's testimony further creates a triable issue whether the asbestos in the 9x9 tiles was supplied by defendant. Defendant's evidentiary objections are overruled. On the issues of punitive damages and premises or contractor Liability, defendant satisfied its burden of production by presenting plaintiffs' factually devoid responses to special interrogatories and plaintiffs failed to provide any evidence creating a triable issue. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR # 8600=(220/HEK)
